1.title: Kraków
1.text: The historic heart of Poland. Wawel Castle, the Main Square, Kazimierz, and centuries of royal heritage.

2.title: Tarnów
2.text: A charming city with Renaissance architecture, warm climate, and a beautifully preserved old town.

3.title: Nowy Sącz
3.text: A gateway to the mountains, wooden architecture, and rich regional traditions.

4.title: Zakopane
4.text: The winter capital of Poland. Tatra Mountains, wooden villas, and vibrant highlander culture.

5.title: Oświęcim
5.text: A city of memory and reflection, home to the Auschwitz‑Birkenau Memorial and Museum.

6.title: Wieliczka
6.text: Famous for its UNESCO‑listed Salt Mine — a world of underground chambers and salt sculptures.

7.title: Bochnia
7.text: One of the oldest cities in Poland, known for its historic salt mine and medieval heritage.

1.title: Villages
1.text: Lanckorona, Chochołów, Lipnica Murowana — wooden houses, folk traditions, and peaceful landscapes.

2.title: Landscapes
2.text: Tatra Mountains, Pieniny, Beskids, Dunajec Gorge — dramatic peaks, rivers, forests, and valleys.

1.title: Food & Beverages
1.text: Oscypek, kwaśnica, pierogi, regional cheeses, craft beers, and traditional highlander cuisine.

2.title: Products
2.text: Local crafts, wooden carvings, lacework, pottery, and regional specialties from mountain villages.

1.title: Art
1.text: Sacred art, wooden churches, folk costumes, and vibrant cultural traditions shaped by centuries.

2.title: UNESCO Sites
2.text: Kraków Old Town, Wieliczka Salt Mine, Kalwaria Zebrzydowska, Wooden Churches of Southern Małopolska.

1.title: Craft
1.text: Highlander craftsmanship, woodwork, textiles, and traditional artisan techniques passed down through generations.

2.title: Businesses
2.text: A dynamic region with tourism, technology, education, and local enterprises driving economic growth.

1.title: Venice
1.text: The city of canals, art, and romance. St. Mark’s Square, Doge’s Palace, Rialto Bridge, Murano.

2.title: Verona
2.text: The city of love and Roman‑Scaliger heritage. The Arena, Juliet’s House, historic center.

3.title: Treviso
3.text: A small gem crossed by canals and medieval arcades.

4.title: Padua
4.text: A historic university city with ancient traditions.

5.title: Vicenza
5.text: The city of Palladio, Renaissance elegance, UNESCO heritage.

6.title: Belluno
6.text: Gateway to the Dolomites, pristine nature and spectacular mountain landscapes.

7.title: Rovigo
7.text: Between the Po and Adige rivers, medieval history and the calm atmosphere of Polesine.

1.title: Villages
1.text: Asolo, Marostica, Bassano del Grappa: historic villages, medieval castles, and unique landscapes.

2.title: Landscapes
2.text: Dolomites, Lake Garda, Adriatic coast: enchanting mountains, lakes, hills, and plains.

1.title: Food & Beverages
1.text: Discover the soul of Italian cuisine — from traditional dishes to modern interpretations, all crafted with passion and the finest ingredients.

2.title: Products
2.text: Discover the unique products of Veneto — from traditional crafts to modern innovations.

1.title: Art
1.text: Discover the soul of Italian art — from Renaissance frescoes and sacred church interiors to timeless landscapes shaped by history, light, and beauty.

2.title: UNESCO Sites
2.text: Venezia, Padova, Dolomiti: world heritage sites.

1.title: Craft
1.text: Traditional Italian craftsmanship: ceramics, glassmaking, wood carving, textiles, and timeless artisan techniques passed down through generations.

2.title: Businesses
2.text: Local businesses: from industry and design to agriculture and craftsmanship, a diverse economy rooted in tradition and innovation.
